methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Velo3D is a public-stage company in Robotics & Manufacturing that has raised over $300M to date. The addressable comp universe consists of 11 peers in the sector, including one publicly traded entity (277810.KQ) and eight private companies. Notable private peers include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector lacks a sufficiently deep peer set meeting narrow stage and valuation-tier criteria—zero same-stage, zero adjacent-stage, and zero similar-tier comparables were identified. Relying on an all-sector median across the full 11-peer universe would introduce substantial category drift and prove uninformative for a public-stage manufacturing robotics player.

The analysis cannot support a specific valuation range given the methodological constraints. The single public comparable (277810.KQ) is too narrow a dataset for standalone application, and the eight private comparables span heterogeneous stages and funding profiles without sufficient homogeneity for triangulation. Key uncertainties include the exact current market capitalization of Velo3D post-public transition, relative margins and growth profiles versus the identified peers, and whether the public comparable operates in sufficiently analogous markets. Practitioners would need to supplement this sector-level analysis with transaction multiples, DCF anchoring to company-specific unit economics, or precedent IPO valuations within the robotics subsector to establish defensible guidance.
